channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: [Side B] Should a Binary-Only FS Support Text Mode? Redrawing the Architecture Boundary

item.title: [Side A] SQLite's Shared In-Memory DB Disappears — and `deserialize()` Won't Save You
item.description: From the Author: D-MemFS was featured in Python Weekly Issue #737 (March 19, 2026) under Interesting Projects, Tools and Libraries. Being picked up by one of the most widely-read Python...

item.title: AIMock: One Mock Server For Your Entire AI Stack
item.description: TL;DR Our CI was flaky, our tests hit live APIs, and every run burned tokens unnecessarily. So the CopilotKit team built AIMock - a mock server that covers your entire agentic stack so your tests...

item.title: 5 Burning Questions About Elon Musk’s Terafab Chip Partnership with Intel
item.description: Intel’s role in Elon Musk’s ambitious chip venture is still murky, raising questions about what the partnership actually entails—and whether it can work at all.

item.title: Handling missing dict keys, revisited
item.description: A while back, I wrote a post showing how to handle missing dict keys. In summary: Using setdefault Using defaultdict Implementing __missing__ While the advice there still stands, I've been reading...

item.title: Agent loops are eating your API budget
item.description: Everyone's shipping agents right now. ReAct, tool-calling loops, whatever. Looks great in demos. But nobody mentions the billing dashboard the morning after. Agent loops are entirely...

item.title: When to Stop Self-Hosting Whisper (and What You Actually Gain)
item.description: Overview This article examines the real costs of self-hosting OpenAI's Whisper versus using AssemblyAI's managed API. It explores the trade-offs between infrastructure control and operational...

item.title: A Vector Database Is Not a RAG Pipeline -And Confusing the Two Will Cost You
item.description: I remember the moment my first RAG setup “worked.” I had a Python script that manually chunked a few PDFs, hit the OpenAI embedding API, shoved the vectors into a local database, and stitched the...

item.title: Anthropic’s New Product Aims to Handle the Hard Part of Building AI Agents
item.description: Amid rapid enterprise growth, Anthropic is trying to lower the barrier to entry for businesses to build AI agents with Claude.

item.title: How I Cut a Telemedicine MVP Down to Something a Clinic Could Actually Use
item.description: A lot of telemedicine products start the same way. A founder says they want to build “a telemedicine app.” Then version one starts sounding like this: Patient App Doctor Dashboard Admin Panel...

item.title: Microsoft’s executive shake-up continues as developer division chief resigns
item.description: Microsoft is losing another veteran executive. Julia Liuson, head of Microsoft's developer division (DevDiv), is resigning from the software giant after 34 years. Liuson spent the past 12 years...

item.title: Add Authentication and MFA to Unreal Engine with Descope
item.description: This blog was originally published on Descope Unreal Engine (UE) is widely known for its ability to create high-fidelity, immersive gaming experiences. Some of the most popular games today have...

item.title: Custom Skills: The End-to-End Workflow Made Executable
item.description: I Was Repeating Myself By the time the harness was working and I'd moved to on-the-loop development, my sessions with Claude had a rhythm. Pick up a Jira ticket. Read the requirements. Decide...

item.title: Masjesu Botnet Emerges as DDoS-for-Hire Service Targeting Global IoT Devices
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have lifted the curtain on a stealthy botnet that's designed for distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks. Called Masjesu, the botnet has been advertised via Telegram...

item.title: Meta is reentering the AI race with a new model called Muse Spark
item.description: Meta Superintelligence Labs is launching its first model since Mark Zuckerberg spent billions overhauling the company's AI efforts. Called Muse Spark, the model now powers the Meta AI app and the...

item.title: Astropad’s Workbench reimagines remote desktop for AI agents, not IT support
item.description: Astropad’s Workbench lets users remotely monitor and control AI agents on Mac Minis from iPhone or iPad, with low-latency streaming and mobile access.

item.title: Amazon’s Smart Thermostat can help lower your energy bills, and it’s down to $62
item.description: With temperatures on the rise and Earth Day right around the corner, now’s a good time to invest in a smart thermostat if you haven’t already. A good one like Amazon’s Smart Thermostat can help...

item.title: OpenAI releases a new safety blueprint to address the rise in child sexual exploitation
item.description: OpenAI's new Child Safety Blueprint aims to tackle the alarming rise in child sexual exploitation linked to advancements in AI.

item.title: Databricks co-founder wins prestigious ACM award, says ‘AGI is here already’
item.description: Matei Zaharia has won the top honor from the Association for Computing Machinery. Now he's working on AI for research and says AGI is simply misunderstood.

item.title: The Miniature Wife was an exercise in visual trickery
item.description: In Manuel Gonzales' 2013 short story "The Miniature Wife," a woman starts to become a different kind of person after her husband accidentally shrinks her down to the size of a coffee mug. Because...

item.title: ALTK‑Evolve: On‑the‑Job Learning for AI Agents
Final 3 days to save up to $500 on your TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 pass
item.description: Save up to $500 on your TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 pass until April 10, 11:59 p.m. PT. Secure your spot at the center of the tech ecosystem. Register today.

item.title: Mustafa Suleyman: AI development won’t hit a wall anytime soon—here’s why
item.description: We evolved for a linear world. If you walk for an hour, you cover a certain distance. Walk for two hours and you cover double that distance. This intuition served us well on the savannah. But it...

item.title: APT28 Deploys PRISMEX Malware in Campaign Targeting Ukraine and NATO Allies
item.description: The Russian threat actor known as APT28 (aka Forest Blizzard and Pawn Storm) has been linked to a fresh spear-phishing campaign targeting Ukraine and its allies to deploy a previously undocumented...

item.title: The vibes are off at OpenAI
item.description: OpenAI is in a relatively precarious position. The company is and has been a funding behemoth - just over a week ago, it closed $122 billion in funding at a post-money valuation of $852 billion....

item.title: Atlassian launches visual AI tools and third-party agents in Confluence
item.description: Confluence users can now create visual assets within the software in addition to new third-party agents working with Lovable, Replit, and Gamma.

item.title: Tractive’s new dog and cat trackers provide more health insights for your pets
item.description: After acquiring its competitor Whistle from Mars Petcare nearly a year ago, Tractive is introducing two new pet trackers today. The Cat 6 Mini features a sleeker design than Tractive's current...

item.title: Xbox’s new era needs games like Forza Horizon 6
item.description: It's a weird time at Xbox. Microsoft's gaming division is under new management, with its current leadership intent on what it describes as "a renewed commitment to Xbox." But given that no one...

item.title: The only USB-C AA battery I’d buy for myself is the Zepath 3600
item.description: Last September, a company named Lumafield scanned 1,000 cylindrical lithium-ion batteries to shine a light on the hidden risks of cheaping out. At roughly the same time, I found myself testing two...

item.title: The Download: water threats in Iran and AI’s impact on what entrepreneurs make
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Desalination plants in the Middle East are increasingly...
